description: A geometric diagram showing the angular coordinate system for v-parameterization in diffusion models. It visualizes a unit circle where z_phi is a point on the circle defined by angle phi, with x and epsilon as axes, and the DDIM update step shown as movement along the -v_hat direction tangent to the circle.
teaching_value: Helps students visualize the abstract math of v-parameterization by grounding it in geometry: the noisy latent z_phi lives on a unit circle, and denoising corresponds to angular rotation, making the velocity parameterization intuitive rather than purely algebraic.
when_to_show: Show when introducing v-parameterization, immediately after presenting the equations phi_t = arctan(sigma_t/alpha_t) and v_phi = alpha_t*epsilon - sigma_t*x, to give geometric intuition for why this parameterization is natural.
suggested_caption: DDIM update as angular rotation on unit circle (Salimans & Ho, 2022)

description: A detailed architecture diagram of the 3D U-Net used in Video Diffusion Models (VDM). Shows the encoder-decoder structure with skip connections, input channels for noisy video z_t, conditioning c, and log-SNR lambda_t, with channel multipliers M1 through MK across layers. Spatial and temporal attention/convolution blocks are labeled.
teaching_value: Concretely illustrates how a standard 2D U-Net is extended to handle video (4D tensors) by factorizing spatial and temporal operations, helping students understand the architectural choices that make video diffusion tractable.
when_to_show: Show when explaining VDM architecture, after describing the factorization of spatial and temporal layers, to give students a concrete reference for how inputs flow through the network.
suggested_caption: 3D U-Net architecture for video diffusion with spatial-temporal factorization (Salimans & Ho, 2022)

description: A pipeline diagram of Imagen Video's cascaded diffusion model system. Shows 7 sequential models: a base video diffusion model followed by alternating spatial super-resolution (SSR) and temporal super-resolution (TSR) stages, progressively increasing resolution and frame rate. Text embeddings are shown injected at each stage.
teaching_value: Illustrates the cascaded generation strategy where quality is built up progressively through specialized models, helping students understand why cascading is used and how spatial vs. temporal upsampling are separated concerns.
when_to_show: Show when introducing Imagen Video, to help students grasp the full pipeline before diving into individual component architectures.
suggested_caption: Imagen Video's 7-model cascaded pipeline for high-resolution video generation (Ho et al., 2022)

description: A detailed block diagram of a single space-time separable block in Imagen Video. Shows the internal structure with 2D spatial convolution, temporal convolution, spatial attention, and temporal attention sub-modules arranged in sequence, with residual connections.
teaching_value: Shows students exactly how spatial and temporal operations are interleaved within a single building block, making the concept of space-time separability concrete and demonstrating how temporal modeling is added to existing spatial architectures.
when_to_show: Show after the Imagen Video pipeline overview, when explaining how each diffusion model in the cascade processes video data internally.
suggested_caption: Space-time separable block combining spatial and temporal attention layers (Ho et al., 2022)

description: An architecture diagram of Sora showing its Diffusion Transformer (DiT) design. Illustrates how video and image inputs are compressed into a latent space, tokenized into spacetime patches, processed by transformer blocks with conditioning on text embeddings, and decoded back to pixel space.
teaching_value: Contrasts the transformer-based approach to video diffusion against U-Net approaches, helping students understand how spacetime patch tokenization enables scalable video generation and why DiT is architecturally different from 3D U-Net models.
when_to_show: Show when transitioning from U-Net-based video diffusion models to transformer-based approaches, to highlight the architectural contrast and motivate why Sora chose DiT.
suggested_caption: Sora's Diffusion Transformer processes video as spacetime patch tokens (Brooks et al., 2024)

description: A pipeline diagram of Make-A-Video showing how a pre-trained text-to-image model is extended for video generation. Illustrates the components: a prior network, decoder, and spatiotemporal super-resolution layers, with arrows showing how text input flows through to produce video output.
teaching_value: Demonstrates the 'inflate and fine-tune' paradigm for video generation, showing students how existing text-image knowledge is reused and where temporal components are inserted, making the transfer learning strategy concrete.
when_to_show: Show when introducing the approach of adapting pre-trained image diffusion models for video, as the first concrete example of this paradigm before discussing pseudo-3D layers in detail.
suggested_caption: Make-A-Video extends text-to-image diffusion with temporal layers for video (Singer et al., 2022)

description: A side-by-side diagram showing how pseudo-3D convolution (left) and pseudo-3D attention (right) layers work. Left panel shows a 2D spatial conv followed by a 1D temporal conv. Right panel shows 2D spatial attention followed by 1D temporal attention, with tensor reshape operations (flatten/unflatten) illustrated.
teaching_value: Provides a clear visual explanation of the pseudo-3D decomposition trick, showing students exactly how spatial and temporal operations are separated and how tensor dimensions are manipulated, making the mathematical formulas in the text tangible.
when_to_show: Show immediately when explaining pseudo-3D layers in Make-A-Video, alongside the equations for h_conv and h_attn, to help students visualize the dimension swapping and flattening operations.
suggested_caption: Pseudo-3D conv and attention decompose spatial and temporal processing sequentially (Singer et al., 2022)

description: An architecture overview of Tune-A-Video showing two stages: a fine-tuning stage on a single reference video and a sampling stage for new video generation. Highlights which layers are updated (full temporal self-attention T-Attn, query projections in ST-Attn and Cross-Attn) versus frozen, with color coding.
teaching_value: Illustrates the one-shot video tuning concept and the selective fine-tuning strategy, helping students understand how to preserve pre-trained image knowledge while adapting for temporal consistency with minimal data.
when_to_show: Show when discussing fine-tuning strategies for video diffusion, particularly to contrast full fine-tuning vs. selective layer updates and explain why only certain projections are updated.
suggested_caption: Tune-A-Video selectively fine-tunes temporal and query layers for one-shot video editing (Wu et al., 2023)

description: A training pipeline diagram for Gen-1 by Runway. Shows how video structure (depth/pose) and content (CLIP embeddings) are separately extracted and used as conditioning signals in a U-Net diffusion model, illustrating the structure-content decomposition approach.
teaching_value: Concretely shows how Gen-1 disentangles structure and content for controllable video editing, helping students understand how different conditioning modalities (structural priors vs. semantic embeddings) are combined in a diffusion framework.
when_to_show: Show when explaining Gen-1's approach to video editing, to illustrate how the abstract decomposition p(x|s,c) is implemented architecturally.
suggested_caption: Gen-1 conditions video generation on separate structure and content signals (Esser et al., 2023)

description: A diagram showing how a pre-trained 2D LDM image model is extended to Video LDM by adding temporal layers. Shows the encoder processing frames independently in spatial dimensions, with temporal attention/convolution layers inserted to model frame-to-frame dependencies, and an optional context frame c_S.
teaching_value: Illustrates the specific architectural modification of inserting temporal layers into a frozen image LDM, helping students understand how minimal changes to an existing model can enable video generation while preserving image generation quality.
when_to_show: Show when explaining Video LDM's approach, to contrast with Make-A-Video and Tune-A-Video and highlight the common theme of temporal layer insertion across different methods.
suggested_caption: Video LDM extends image LDM by inserting temporal layers into encoder-decoder (Blattmann et al., 2023)

description: Training pipeline diagram for the autoencoder in Video LDM, showing a frozen encoder processing image sequences and a fine-tuned decoder with a new temporal discriminator that enforces cross-frame consistency across T frames.
teaching_value: Illustrates the asymmetric fine-tuning strategy where only the decoder is updated with temporal layers while the encoder stays frozen, helping students understand how image LDMs are efficiently adapted for video without retraining the full model.
when_to_show: Show when explaining how Video LDM adapts a pretrained image LDM for video — specifically to contrast which components are frozen vs. fine-tuned and why.
suggested_caption: Video LDM autoencoder: frozen encoder, temporally fine-tuned decoder. (Blattmann et al. 2023)

description: Architectural comparison diagram showing how Lumiere replaces a temporal super-resolution (TSR) cascade with an inflated SSR (spatial super-resolution) network that processes overlapping short video segments to handle memory constraints.
teaching_value: Clarifies the design trade-off in Lumiere: eliminating TSR simplifies the pipeline but requires processing overlapping segments due to memory limits, helping students understand how architecture choices are driven by computational constraints.
when_to_show: Show when contrasting Lumiere's architecture against Video LDM/SVD, especially when discussing how TSR is removed and what replaces it in the generation pipeline.
suggested_caption: Lumiere replaces TSR with inflated SSR on overlapping video segments. (Bar-Tal et al. 2024)

description: Three-panel architecture diagram of Lumiere's Space-Time U-Net (STUNet): (a) the full U-Net with spatial and temporal downsampling/upsampling paths, (b) a convolution-based block with factorized space-time convolution appended to pretrained layers, and (c) an attention-based block with temporal attention appended at the coarsest level.
teaching_value: Shows concretely how a pretrained text-to-image U-Net is inflated into a space-time model by inserting factorized temporal convolutions and temporal attention, making the inflation strategy tangible and distinguishing which layers are pretrained vs. newly trained.
when_to_show: Show when teaching how pretrained image diffusion U-Nets are extended to video via temporal layer insertion — ideal for comparing with Video LDM's approach and explaining factorized convolution.
suggested_caption: STUNet inflates a text-to-image U-Net with temporal convolution and attention. (Bar-Tal et al. 2024)

description: Pipeline overview of Text2Video-Zero showing how a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model generates temporally consistent video frames without any training, using motion-enriched latent initialization and cross-frame attention replacing standard self-attention.
teaching_value: Demonstrates that training-free video generation is possible by manipulating the latent space and attention mechanism of an existing image model, helping students understand zero-shot adaptation and the role of cross-frame attention in temporal consistency.
when_to_show: Show when introducing training-free adaptation methods, especially to contrast with fine-tuning approaches and to explain how motion information can be injected into latent codes without retraining.
suggested_caption: Text2Video-Zero generates videos from a frozen image diffusion model. (Khachatryan et al. 2023)

description: Overview diagram of ControlVideo, illustrating how ControlNet is applied per-frame at each diffusion timestep to condition video generation on structural inputs (e.g., depth maps, edges), with outputs added to the main U-Net skip connections.
teaching_value: Shows how ControlNet's per-frame conditioning integrates into a training-free video diffusion pipeline, helping students understand how spatial control signals are applied consistently across frames without additional training.
when_to_show: Show when discussing how Text2Video-Zero is extended with ControlNet, or when explaining how structural conditioning (depth, edges) can guide video generation in a training-free setting.
suggested_caption: ControlVideo applies ControlNet per-frame for training-free structured video generation. (Zhang et al. 2023)
